This game should be filed under the dictionary definition of missed potential.

Lets wind back time and look at where this all began, pubg is released in 2017 and dominates the market with new battle royal game, literally gets everyone under the bright gaming lights of their 144hz monitors to play it. Gun play is tight but issues with performance and cheating are significant.

4 years later, its suffering from the same issues along with new trouble its invented for itself by not listening to the community. Cheaters, not refining core gameplay, adding features noone wants or asks for, the most expensive scam defining skins in any game these are the defining characteristics of this game, and really its a shame because under all this ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ is some of the best gun play of any fps on the market.

Honestly most of these issues I can forgive, as someone who has over 2k hours in it, there is great potential here. The real defining problem, and it is the most significant problem, is "Blue Balls" aka the brain dead developers. They have never listened to their community, ever since after like a month of the game being out the community recognised that the red zones should be removed.

Simply trimming the bad maps, adding new mechanics to ensure safer rotations, a focus on meds and ammo instead of wasted spawns, most of these fixes that are suggested by the pilars of the community as simply done with a quick patch, yet the devs just double down on new maps, $500 skins, and patches that always break the game.

TL;DR: Save yourself the 30 bucks and ring up that old pot smoking friend from highschool who still lives in his mums basement, because that moment where you hear their voice crack as they recognise they haven't changed in 10 years is the same thing you'll feel in 4 years if you are still playing this pile of ♥♥♥♥.

review_text:
Posted: May 27, 2017
EARLY ACCESS REVIEW
Introduction
PLAYERUNKNOWN'S BATTLEGROUNDS (PUBG) is a battle-royale, 100-players, open-world MMOFPS/TPS set on a massive 8x8km land. The game supports solo, duo, and squad (3-4 ppl) play. Gameplay is very straightforward, you hop off a plane, loot weapons, armors, and equipments from buildings, and survive the thrilling PvP bloodbath.

PUBG is obviously not the first massive battle-royale game out there, yet it stands on the pinnacle of its genre. As you may have known, PUBG has had a highly successful launch, securing a place on Steam's Top Seller game. If you're looking for a good battle-royale PvP, look no further. You've struck the gold.

My claim is not without reason. The following is PUBG's pros and cons in my opinion:

1. Extremely active and communicative developers. This is vital for an early access game. PUBG is being constantly updated every month. I would speculate that this is one of the major factors in PUBG's success. Other battle-royale PvP games are mostly in Early-Alpha for years and their devs are too focused on gathering as much money as they can from the current players without making any real development progress on the game - PUBG is exactly the opposite.

2. Great gameplay concept. No pay-to-win and no grind-to-win. Apparels are only cosmetics. The only thing you keep from playing PUBG is your skill. The PvP is thrilling and definitely fun to play with your friends.

3. Non-toxic community. Simply because there is no chat function. Switch your voice channel to team-only and you're good to go. I wouldn't recommend teaming up with strangers because of obvious reasons.

4. Highly demanding on teamwork and strategy. You can't just hop off the plane and recklessly run around the map no-scoping bozos. You have to plan out your movement very carefully and quickly since time is of the essence. Difficult decisions comes up often, especially in squad play (e.g. risk your life to revive your mate or run like hell to safety; Kill someone and give out your position to nearby players or stay quiet; Deciding your next 'safe spot', etc.) An enjoyable learning curve definitely exists - not too steep and not too lean.

5. A shooting game that applies semi-realistic weapon recoil, bullet travel time, and bullet drop is always appreciated.

6. Various vehicles, weapons, weapon mods, clothings, and equipments are scattered all over the battleground to aid you in your quest of survival. The weapon stats variety amps up the gameplay - It can be quite a challenge to engage in a firefight when you got the wrong weapon for the situation.

7. Although FPS is not recommended due to limited POV, the game supports both FPS and TPS.

8. Minor performance improvements can get quite noticeable after several updates. Devs truly are working on the optimization issue. We can look forward towards better performance in the future.

1. Currently, very performance-demanding. The game isn't optimized that well yet and runs on very unstable fps even on high-end rigs. The minimum 6 gigs of RAM is an absolute minimum - virtual RAMs can't help your 4-gigs-RAM PC. In terms of GPU, one of my friends that is using a GTX980 is suffering from <30fps and extremely slow loading progress. Another friend with a GTX1060 suffers from fps drops during rain/smoke.

As a response to comments' feedback for my 1st cons list: The game is still playable on all those mentioned GPUs. I just want you to know that the game is not optimized well. FPS drops, crashes, and screen freeze will sometimes happen even on high-end rigs. It happens randomly - I don't measure the frequency.



2. Server is lagging badly due to high player base. I would speculate devs didn't expect a huge sale boom and didn't have the resources to support all the players. As an example, you were running to cover when under enemy fire. You already reached the cover, but 2 seconds later, blood spurts out of your chest, you fell down to the ground gasping for air as your vision starts to blur. You scream for your squadmate to magically heal your bullet wound by rubbing the grass around you, but then...okay you get my point

3. Gameplay is monotonous. Similar to CSGO's Competitive and Dota 2, you only have one type of game. The unique experience relies on various encounters with the squad/enemy in-game.

4. Melee weapons are terribly inaccurate. Aiming also can be a bit off sometimes. Possibly caused by the server lag.

5. Controls are terribly not reactive. It takes some miliseconds for the game to recognize LMB/RMB/'Use' command. Or even failing to recognize the commands at all. It can take three, four RMBs to activate the scope. It's kind of annoying when the weapon refuses to shoot when I press LMB because of the input recognition lag. This is a vital issue for a PvP game - devs better fix this quickly.

As a response to the comments' feedback: I have to add that my statement about the terrible control is a rare occasion. Generally, 1, or 2 key inputs would be recognized. I would say having to press twice is much more common than 3/4 times like I said. But it does often take some miliseconds for the inputs to be recognized. My point is still valid: The controls are NOT reactive - horrible for a PvP game.

6. Vehicles flips too easily and difficult/too unrealistic to drive

7. Door and movement bugs seem to be quite prevalent lately. Whenever I move a step ahead, the game throws me back 3, 4 steps backwards. Seems to be affecting the entire server whenever it happens. Invisible doors also prevent players from entering a building, throwing us outdoors whenever we try to enter a building. Similar to fps spikes, these happen randomly.

Don't lose all hopes because of my cons list - remember that this is an early access game. I know some of the cons can be too much to handle and disturbs the gameplay, but devs are very active in updating the game. I dare to conclude that PUBG is currently the best battle-royale MMOFPS/TPS PvP game out there. The current state of the game is more than playable. Definitely worth purchasing even when it's not on sale.

Well that's all I have to say about PUBG. Hope it can be of help to potential buyers!



UPDATE: Never expected my review would go up top on Most Helpful for this game. So I've decided to take down this review after having more playtime hours. I'll update it if there're any significant changes.

UPDATE 2: This review was made before the era of the 'stream sniping' and the microtransaction drama. I haven't played the game for a while so I lack proper first-hand experience to update my review. With that said, keep in mind that this review is outdated.

review_text:
Posted: February 28, 2024
After investing over 2500 hours into the game and purchased the game before it became free, I regret to express my inability to recommend it to prospective players. The prevalence of cheating on the OC servers during peak times is alarmingly pervasive, with multiple cheating teams active in each match. While it remains feasible to prevail against cheaters, the experience is markedly frustrating.

The consequences for caught cheaters are notably lenient, typically resulting in bans spanning from 24 hours to a maximum of three days, with permanent bans being exceptionally rare occurrences.

The development team's focus on cosmetic updates, while aesthetically pleasing, unfortunately detracts from addressing the pressing issue of cheating. By prioritizing the mitigation of cheating, the game stands to retain and engage a larger player base, consequently fostering increased investment in cosmetics.

Furthermore, the repercussions of attempting to expose cheating through platforms such as YouTube, as evidenced by the flagging and subsequent banning of my friend's YouTube account by Krafton.

I implore the developers to reevaluate their priorities and allocate resources towards effectively combating cheating, thereby safeguarding the integrity and longevity of the game for its dedicated community.

Thanks,

Hak

review_text:
Posted: October 2, 2019
Boy oh boy, here we go.
I don't feel as "qualified" to write this review as some, because of my comparatively low amount of hours played. But, I have been playing since early 2017 when the game was first becoming popular.

PUBG is an extremely polarizing game. It seems that you either hate it or you don't. There are many reasons for this. This review is mainly focused on giving you a snapshot of one player's experience (myself, obviously), and my ultimate verdict on whether or not this game is worth your 30 USD.

The concept of the game is great. If you don't like battle royales, don't play it. If you like battle royales, you might like it. Simple as that.

PUBG has a focus on realistic, skill-based weapon handling and gameplay. Obviously, it's not a simulator in the sense of realism, but the weapon physics - bullet drop, bullet velocity, shotgun spread, etc. - is quite reasonable. It's a game that is difficult to learn, let alone master, unless you've played a lot of games with similar gameplay, like DayZ or Arma 3.

I'm not going to go into the variety of weapons, maps, and tactics in this review. The main purpose is to address the primary issues people have with the game. From my experience (which includes reading many other reviews), these issues are: hackers, lag/desync, poor optimization, and bad game support. Let's break them down one by one.

PUBG has always had a major hacker problem. If you've been following the game for a while like I have, you know that PUBG Corp (the company that now owns the game) has been fighting hackers since release. Hundreds of thousands, maybe millions, have been banned during the game's lifetime. There was a time when you would get killed by someone using aimbot at least half of the time. I remember a game where I looked at the kill feed and suddenly saw there were only 15 people left - 2 minutes into the game. Then I got hit with a shotgun. Watching the deathcam, I saw a naked man holding a shotgun, spinning rapidly in all directions, shooting wildly, halfway across the map. Killing everyone. Hacking is a problem.
But - but! It is not as big of a problem as it used to be. You will read reviews that say that "oh I die to hackers literally 90% of the time" and stuff like that. I don't want to slander anyone, but I have a really hard time believing those reviews. In the last three months, I have played about a 900 games (the game tracks your stats closely). I have been killed by an obvious hacker twice. TWICE. I reported them both times, and, both times, I got a message from the game a few days later telling me that the player I had reported had been banned.
The inherent problem is that many people, I think, don't understand the difference between being really good and hacking. It's easy to cry AIMBOT! at someone who killed you with a full-auto burst from an assault rifle at 100 meters, but maybe that person is a god-tier player with 3000+ hours under their belt? My experience: hacking is no longer the huge, gamebreaking issue it once was, and should not be something that stops you from buying and playing the game.

Next is lag/desync. This, I have also had a history of problems with. I have lost games because my game desynced. Is this the fault of the developers, or my own crappy internet connection? I don't know. What I do know is that, in the last three months, I have experienced major desync maybe once. Maybe. And I don't get lag anymore. I think that your own internet connection has a much bigger chance of ruining the game with lag and desync, and I don't think that the game itself is broken in this way.

Now for poor optimization. This one is big for me. This game is terribly optimized, especially for FPS. My GTX 1080 can't even give me 70 FPS at moderate settings. I haven't been able to find a fix. Many others have this problem or others. I don't see any justification for how a game this popular that has been out for this long can still be so poorly optimized. This one is tough, but I deal with it because I like the game.

And, finally, poor development support. I won't go into this one much, because everyone has different opinions on what the developer should update or change next. I will say that PUBG Corp cares about money more than its players, and they usually focus or releasing new (obscenely expensive for what they are) skins over new content.

In conclusion, while PUBG is a flawed game that will always be divisive, I recommend it. The issues I have mentioned are usually overblown by people who hate the game, in my experience. Give it a try, and who knows, you might get hooked. But if you stop playing after 3 hours, I don't think it will be because of any of the big issues. It will be because it just isn't the right game for you.

Thank you for reading this review; I hope it helps you.

Posted: January 22, 2020
PUBG was a great game. The hacking in this game is still a huge problem and has made most of the people i played it with to give up on the game and move onto something else. It was great fun playing with friends, great maps and gameplay. But i feel like those days have passed and until the developers find a way to stop the hacking all together theres no point playing the game. Watching your deathcam and seeing hackers lock on and know exactly where you are when they shouldn't nearly every match you play takes it toll. I hope that one day its fixed but its been a problem for such a long time now that i think its unlikely to ever happen. Sucks because everytime i see an update, a new map or new guns im always keen to give it another go but the hackers just ruin the experience everytime.

review_text:
Posted: 16 December, 2019
Well..

Ok, Ill admit, unlike other high profile folk that have more hours then me.. I have just under 2000 hours but have been playing this game for well over 10 years.. To be exact March 8th 2009. (as my badge says)

I may be a no one in the community, but I have been supporting this game since I first played. The game I would always come back to and call "home".

I, over the years have made friends, enemies, servers.. Learnt different modes, meta, styles, languages.. I have been through multiple events like, The Sniper vs. Spy Update, Classless Update, the big 119th updates update (no joke..) Solider vs Demoman, Scream Fortresses, Christmas updates! You get the point..

There is no sparkle to this game anymore, I cannot jump on a 118 ping New York server, late at night waiting for the Americans to get back online in the evening, me and Banksy taking on 2Fort as Demoman to kill all soliders so that the Demoman could have his own weapon? (Spolier: Solider won, welcome to the Gunboats..) Then finding out TF2 came onto Mac and people that had it, got the ear buds, that sold well..

To be fair, I could go on and tell my war stories, but that feeling when the family gets a new puppy and you get shut out as no one is interested in you anymore? This is exactly whats happening with TF2 (dev side).. I always check steam stats and there is always over 50k people playing, always top 10 on the chart but something is missing..

As part of the community, us loyal people that cherish this game, we are the backbone to this beautiful creation. You don't see Blizzard (well not all the time) not maintaining their beautiful creation and of course World of Warcraft is older then this.. Maybe because of the paid subscription, but I am sure that TF2 alone use to get more money as a free to play game at one point. (Jeeze I had to pay for TF2 over 10 years ago..)

I mean ok, fair play, you have a new game coming out.. whooptie doo.. Send all the cavalry towards that.. Yet instead to the people who grew up with TF2, old players and new players. People who have spent hundreds and even thousands of dollars for now a game which to me is dying.. Not the people's fault, but your own selves..
Jumping into a casual game (YOUR servers) to be greeted by not one sniper but three sniper "bots" that have their own way-points, spamming "good shot mate", aim botting, auto spamming chat so no one can see whats written as a wall of blank appears and calling no when someone votes them a ban. When there is 3 or more of them they win the no vote anyway..

When making your own stuff glitch gets patched.. finding glitches in the items_game text file gets patched.. Oh yeah I forgot that's your profits going down the drain.. But don't worry as bots and hackers are not, so were forget about it.. the game.. the people, who truly love and support this game..

Excuse the rant but for a game that still, to me at this dire moment, is still rocking the steam charts, this should be dealt with within a matter of days or weeks.. There are no new updates like the Solider vs Demoman.. The love for this game from you, just has vanished..

I will carry on playing as I still love this game and class this as home. But for now, don't expect me to dedicate my time or spend money on anything till this "bot" situation and other stupid stuff is dealt with.. and hopefully soon. With new updates that sparkle in my eyes.. And a new bloody weapon for the Demoman as I deemed the solider win was unfair.

Do I recommend it to others? Oh yes, yes I do. But at the moment with the bot situation, other stupid stuff and the game at the moment being "devless". No.
But still download it, go onto the community servers, find a mode, map, server and enjoy. I will give it a thumbs up but really should be a thumbs down at this point of the games life.

**New Players** the first initiate is daunting, but play for fun, learn who the characters are and the history about them. I did and it got me 2000 hours, 10 years of play and a game I could call home. There are also comics about the game to if you like to read.

Good luck and thank you for the read.

review_text:
Posted: December 27, 2025
I never expected to end up writing a review for this game after clocking more than 10 000 hours, but here we are.

Before I even had a Steam account, Team Fortress 2 was already part of my life. I grew up watching gameplay clips, GMod videos, and all kinds of community content that left a lasting impression. By the time TF2 became free-to-play and I finally jumped in myself, it already felt familiar. Now, after playing for nearly a decade, I can confidently say that TF2 is my favorite game of all time. That’s not an easy conclusion to reach given how many incredible games exist, but the realization eventually became unavoidable. It’s like recognizing something that’s been there all along. TF2 was it, and it still is.

Over the years, this game has introduced me to countless people and unforgettable moments. Some sessions were calm and social, just chatting with strangers, while others descended into pure chaos. From friendly Sandvich gatherings to absolute madness on maps like 2Fort, every experience feels genuine and unique. The community is creative, hilarious, expressive, and full of personality.

TF2 also feels like a central hub for multiplayer gaming on Steam. It’s free, iconic, and often serves as an entry point for new players discovering online games. Some move on to other titles, while others always keep TF2 installed, returning again and again.

The core game itself deserves just as much praise. Each of the nine mercenary classes is bursting with personality, memorable voice lines, and distinct playstyles. Their character design is so strong that they feel almost real, supported further by comics, animations, and years of community-created content like SFM and GMod videos. Customization plays a huge role too. From expressive loadouts to completely ridiculous outfits, cosmetic freedom adds another layer of identity and humor. And of course, TF2’s economy became legendary. At its peak, trading was an entire ecosystem of its own, with massive value exchanges and complex bartering that still exists today in more subtle form.

The social rules that formed naturally within TF2 are just as fascinating. Friendly players, unspoken agreements, bizarre traditions, and strange behaviors all contribute to a culture that makes little sense from the outside but feels perfectly normal once you’re part of it. Few games have managed to develop such a unique identity, blending gameplay, lore, humor, and social norms into something that feels almost infinite.

In the end, TF2 stands as a testament to what a dedicated community can build around a well-designed foundation. Through custom modes, films, memes, friendships, and years of shared experiences, the game has grown far beyond its original scope. Its influence stretches across gaming culture and the internet as a whole. If longevity and pure fun matter, it’s hard to think of another game that offers so much to so many people.

Whether you’re diving into competitive matches, relaxing on community servers, or just messing around with friends, TF2 delivers. It’s strange, energetic, endlessly entertaining, and still very much alive. And considering it’s free to play, there’s no reason not to experience it!

review_text:
Posted: January 23, 2017
In a land a long, long time ago, Team Fortress 2 would be a critically acclaimed first person shooter. Boasting its colorful characters against the dreary palette of the Badlands, this game offered to many a wonderful experience. Later updates would add cosmetics, weapons, and a ton of other things to sink your teeth into. Some of those updates are not as well viewed by the community as others, like the addition of crazier and crazier hats. In recent years, the community has felt somewhat negatively towards the team of developers and their updates for this game. Though, there is something important to remember...

We first saw those deranged mercenaries nearly a decade ago! This game has been alive and kicking for a long time, and I doubt that you will soon see a day where these mercs aren't defying laws of physics, common sense, and sometimes all laws in general. You won't find many other games with a soviet in love with a 300 pound minigun that he just lugs around like it's nothing. Nor will you find a quick-witted, caffeine-fueled Bostonian who dances around the battlefield, dodging rockets and bullets like a madman. This game is very outlandish, and incredibly enjoyable. Even after years and years of pushing a payload down a track to blow up the enemies... people want to come back, because although there's some basis and objectives for the game modes, you never play the same game twice. Every match is a total surprise. Even competitively, the 9 classes and variety in what you can play with, it will give you a new experience nearly every time you play.

Years of pushing a cart filled to the brim with explosives is a hard task. Thankfully, there are new faces, hats to wear, weapons to try, and a new experience to be had every single day..

review_text:
Posted: October 12, 2014
My hours. help.

I wrote something for real!

These hours are legit, I played this game a lot in the past to avoid some tragic stuff in real life. I was young and didn't really have my gaming consoles anymore because of my brothers and I had a really bad pc, which i still own. TF2 was one of the games i could play pretty well on this pc but with horrid graphics, but it was one of the more fun games I installed. I met some real cool and nice people on this game as well as people who i plan to keep by my side. The community back then was more humble compared to nowadays. You have competitive gamers who spam their bind keys with unfunny/gross jokes and very obnoxious people who are either yelling at you or telling you to do specific things to win the game. When really, it doesn't even matter if you win or lose. However, you also have those people who are really awesome and you may meet some nice friends along the way! Although you never know what kinda person you're going to meet on this game. In my honest opinion, if you want to have a game to just waste time on (and money) or just mess around with people you know (which is what I do now), this is perfect. Esspecially the mann vs. machine co-op. I mainly play that now. Although to me, the game lost its touch, update wise, and I moved onto other games.

I believe it's worth a shot to try it out but other than that, I'd keep it as a casual game. I don't think the competitive scene is worth getting into anymore because it's being flooded by crude people. Atleast from my experiance after over about 4+ years of playing it just got worse and worse. You will eventually be tied into drama even if you try to avoid it. You'll be more stressed than actually having fun, I'll tell you that!

Although in all seriousness, this game is losing its touch and probably isn't worth making it your main game MAINLY because the updates they've been releasing are becoming very dull and not as great as they used to be. BUT, it's a great game to just play to pass the time and have fun! All you'll really notice nowdays is...people really love to trade more than actually play. Living off as a free to play would be very tough and you'd need to be patient with items you would want, unless you got that MONEY! Hope this helps.


One last thing, I am not saying this game is bad in any way. I am saying that it's worth trying and playing with friends, or even meeting people! Although there are some downsides that you won't like, such as some stuff I pointed out above. Strictly towards the community. I personally think you should have this game as a side game rather than your come home to play sorta thing. Have it as a once in a while game, because it's nothing to be big about, unless you really do not like most of the millions of other games out there.

Just give it a shot and see what you think! Have fun <3

review_text:
I am writing to share a disappointing experience with Amazon regarding a Black Friday order.

I placed an order during Black Friday with an estimated delivery date in June and patiently waited for several months. However, I later discovered that the order had been canceled in April due to a declined payment method.

What makes this situation particularly frustrating is that the original payment was successfully authorized via my Visa card at the time of purchase. Due to the long delay before Amazon attempted to capture the payment, a re-authorization was required. Unfortunately, I was never notified or given the opportunity to update or confirm my payment details.

Had I been informed earlier, I would have purchased the item from another retailer that was offering a slightly higher price at the time. Now, that price is no longer available, and I have effectively lost the opportunity entirely.

When I contacted customer support, I was simply told that the order could not be reinstated, without any meaningful attempt to resolve the issue or offer compensation.

This experience reflects poorly on how Amazon handles long-term orders and payment re-authorizations. At the very least, customers should be given a fair chance to resolve payment issues—especially after waiting for months.

I hope Amazon takes this feedback seriously and improves its communication and customer support processes in similar situat

review_text:
I purchased a defective keyboard through Amazon from a third party seller called LowFree. The battery was dead on arrival. I contacted Amazon customer service multiple times to resolve a simple return and refund situation. What followed was one of the most frustrating experiences I have ever had with any company.

Agents repeatedly hung up on me. Every time I called back I had to start from zero and explain everything again. I was bounced between Amazon and the seller with each one pointing at the other. When I specifically requested an A-to-Z Guarantee claim — which is my right as a buyer on their platform — agents told me they did not have that option. That is either a lie or a training failure. Either way it is unacceptable.

The return label Amazon provided came with a $65 shipping fee at DHL for a defective product that was never functional to begin with. I should not be paying a single dollar to return something that arrived broken. When I raised this, agents acknowledged the issue and said it would be reimbursed but refused to put anything in writing or escalate to a manager despite multiple requests.

Amazon is the biggest marketplace in the world. They have a responsibility to protect buyers not just sellers. Their customer service model is built to exhaust you until you give up. Do not give up. File the A-to-Z Guarantee claim, dispute the charge with your bank, and document everything from the first contact.

I will be updating this review with the final outcome.

review_text:
Amazon usually has top notch support and is absolutely amazing with everything, so it's unfortunate that I have to write this review about some changes to their customer service and/or policies surrounding returns.

The other day I ordered a RODE gen 3 lapel wireless mic system because my Webcam doesn't have a mic in it, and I use my MacBook Pro in clamshell mode now for ergonomic reasons, and so the internal mics can't be used anymore.

I later realized that the Rode's AI would likely not be suitable for this, and I found much better options for both Zoom and for recording desktop videos.

One of Amazon's extremely annoying quirks is that anytime ANYTHING has proceeded to the shipping process even if it's just been marked to be shipped, the ability to cancel the order is almost impossible.

And as it stands the order is set to arrive tomorrow. It's been shipped already, cancellation is a guaranteed fail at this point.

I started a chat, and told the agent I wanted to return the item. The agent then said he was going to try to cancel the item. I didn't really question it, even though it is NOT what I asked him to do, because I figured that if he was trying to cancel it, he had confidence it would succeed.

He said he raised a request to cancel it, which means it's the same thing that happens when you try to cancel it yourself.

So I told him I don't want it cancelled, I never asked for that, I want to open a return so when it gets here I can just take it to the UPS store.

The guy must have been awful with English or completely insubordinate because he kept on telling me he was going to try to get the order cancelled, even after I told him over and over again I wanted to return it.

I ended the chat and started a new chat. Then this person said they would return it, but then said they needed to transfer me to a "concerns rep" .. I told that rep I wanted it returned and I know it hasn't arrived yet but that it was NEVER an issue in the past to open a return before the item is delivered and then just take the package to UPS store to drop it off.

I told him this TWICE. Despite this, the agent STILL replied with:

We cannot open a return for an item that has not yet been marked delivered, even though they did this in the past.

I am fed up with companies lying like this and ignoring direct truths stated by customers and that's why I am leaving this scathing review.

I spent 1 hour and a half in chat with them , and NOW I have do it AGAIN!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! After it's delivered and I am IRATE.

I would encourage Amazon to focus on ensuring consistency in customer interactions and empowering supervisors to resolve situations holistically rather than defaulting strictly to policy.

review_text:
Summary: infested property, rotting wood. No refund. Terrible customer service. Refusal to escalate. No offer of resolution other than £59.19 of travel credits that expire within a year, being of no use to us given we would never use Booking.com again.

In detail: Uninhabitable. We booked a property for a few days. Arrived at close to midnight to find the cottage thick with the smell of damp and infested with woodlice. There was wood dust all over the settee, which must have come from disintegrating ceiling beams. We phoned the host to help resolve for advice and all they were willing to offer is "maybe" a cleaner tomorrow which clearly wouldn't have been sufficient as this issue required a damp and mould specialist and pest control; in any case not fit to stay in from a health she safety perspective to the point we felt we ought to sleep in the car if we couldn't find somewhere to stay. At midnight, we had to leave and find alternative accommodation options of which were limited given the time of night, and could only find something 6 miles outside where we needed to be. This cost us an extra £519 plus the cost of additional fuel we had to factor in. We called Booking.com and they refused to issue a refund stating it is the vendor Host and Stay's responsibility. The vendor deflected us to Booking.com. We went round in circles like this 3 times. What the vendor did admit is that the property hadn't been cleaned in two weeks, which apart from not explaining the state of degradation of the property, is in itself an unacceptable level of care. We asked booking.com to escalate to a supervisor as they refused to do anything other than ask us to resolve with host. The customer service advisor put the phone down on us. We have no recourse other than to seek a refund from our bank given the circles we're going in with Booking.com and Host & Stay.

review_text:
I only use Booking to collect points with their partners (e.g., Wizz Air). Otherwise, it’s usually better to book directly with hotel websites, which are often cheaper. In fact, when you call hotels, they sometimes ask for the price listed on Booking and then offer you a lower rate—showing how much commission Booking takes.

My recent experience was extremely frustrating. I booked through their website and was charged a higher price due to exchange rates. They offered free cancellation within 3 hours, and I cancelled within 15 minutes. The money hadn’t even left my account yet, but Booking still plans to collect it and hold it for 7–12 days. I’ll most likely lose money again due to exchange rate differences.

I also reported that they allow scam listings on their platform. After booking a flat on their website, I was asked to make a payment through a website that looked like Booking.com but was clearly fraudulent. Booking didn’t offer any compensation—no voucher, no discount, not even an apology. I only received a generic email saying they would investigate and that my booking was cancelled.

Their customer service is very disappointing—slow and often rude. I’ve had multiple negative experiences, and loyalty levels like Genius 3 don’t seem to make any difference in how customers are treated.

I’m actively looking for alternative platforms and will gladly switch as soon as I find a reliable one.

review_text:
I booked a Turkish Airlines flight from Abu Dhabi to Bordeaux through Booking.com for this month (May 2026).
A (50+yr old) female travelling alone.

When Turkish Airlines wanted to cancel this flight due to the conflict in the Middle East, I wasn’t offered the option to change the flight, which should have been an easy next step for Booking.com.
I had to request this.

I asked for the outbound flight to be changed as it was no longer available.
There was NO discussion about the return leg (which had a 2 hour layover in Istanbul and was fine to stay as is) and was still available.

So the outbound was changed.

What Booking.com didn’t tell me is that they quietly changed the second leg of the inbound flight from Istanbul to Abu Dhabi. Although leaving at the same time from Bordeaux, my short 2 hour daytime layover in Istanbul became a 22 hour nightime layover.

When I noticed the change in the confirmation email they sent me I was horrified and contacted Booking.com to complain.
With no sign of remorse, Booking.com told me I had agreed to it when they sent me the new itinerary to review.
I refused to accept this on the grounds that no one discussed the return leg with me when I only agreed to the change of outbound flight.

After more than two hours on the phone I was eventually apologised to and told someone would contact me within 24 hours to rectify the issue.
That was three weeks ago and still nothing from them at all.

Extremely poor service.

BE CAREFUL WHEN USING BOOKING.COM.
CHECK EVERYTHING AS THEY DON’T INFORM YOU OF SOME CHANGES THEN WILL BLAME YOU FOR NOT NOTICING THEM.

CUSTOMER HAPPINESS IS CLEARLY
NOT ON THEIR LIST OF IMPORTANCE.

Well we suggest you pay the hotel directly for the extra day. Fine. Done. Then I get an email that the booking has been changed to the new dates June 5-9. Excellent. Two days later in the afternoon ‘your booking has been canceled’ Why? I did not cancel it. Then I find an email coming in the middle of the night saying that if I did not reply to this request for confirmation of my new booking it would be canceled. Impossible to get bv AI Katia to a
person without telling her what I wanted to speak to an agentcabout. She/it doesn’t understand complex answers and comes back with ‘is there anything else I can help you with? Finally I invent some answer and then she wants my confirmation number. Fine I find it in the cancelation email. I start the number and before I can finish it she/it says ‘seems like your number doesn’t match, it must be 10 or 12-22 numbers. By this time I am yelling be patient and let me finish’. Wasting my breath she interrupts again ‘your confirmation number must be 10 numbers or 12-22 numbers, over and over again. Finally she let me finish but then she wants a code. The email doesn’t have a code and my reservation has vanished from the Booking.com app. Not possibke to do anything further without the code. So finally the light dawns…I will have to get the confirmation number and code from a different reservation I have with Booking and lie to Katia giving her this other code. She is not so smart and I finally get an agent and explain my lie and he sends me an email that has both the confirmation number and the code. So now we can talk business. Sort of. ‘We can’t reverse a cancellation’ But I didn’t cancel and you have received $2500 in payment from my bank in two differnt payments. What was the second payment for ? ‘Oh that was forthe extra night’! But one of your inmates told me to pay the extra day to the hotel directly, not through Booking.com. Yes but the reservation was canceled and is non-refundable. So I will contact the hotel to see if they will reinstate the reservation but then you will have a double reservation for the 5th of June. Why can’t you just reinstate the original reservation from June 6-9 since another of your inmates told me to do it that way and pay the extra day directly to the hotel? Innate #4 completely bumfuzzled , babbling incoherently. ‘I’ve called the hotel but no answer (2am at the hotel ) so I’ve sent an email to the hotel and you should receive an answer within 24 hours. Meanwhile I’m left wondering where my money is and in which insane asylum Booking.com keeps its employees.

Booking.com's response was equally disappointing. After days of waiting, they offered me 12.57 EUR in Travel Credits as compensation for a 290 EUR theft — less than 5% of my actual loss. I have sent multiple messages and reminders over several days with no meaningful reply. When I asked for help contacting my next property in Stockholm, they provided me with contact details belonging to a previous owner who no longer manages the hotel.
I have a police report, proof of purchase, and a full written record of every interaction. Booking.com collects commission on every booking but takes zero responsibility when something goes seriously wrong.
I would strongly caution anyone against relying on Booking.com's customer support in an emergency. You are completely on your own.

I had a booking cancelled by Booking.com with only 5 hours’ notice the day before I was due to run the London Marathon for charity (Royal British Legion). No explanation was provided and no alternative accommodation was arranged.

When I contacted customer support, I was initially offered just £60 in travel credit despite the cheapest replacement accommodation costing £133 more than my original booking. I then spoke with a manager who promised to support me “every step of the way” but did not arrange accommodation, confirm reimbursement, or follow up as promised.

I was left stranded the night before the marathon and had no choice but to book alternative accommodation myself at increased cost due to last-minute availability. Although my original booking (£137) has been refunded, I am still £133 out of pocket and my follow-up email sent on 29 April requesting reimbursement remains unanswered.

This caused unnecessary stress immediately before a major charity event and the level of customer support was extremely disappointing.

I am still waiting for Booking.com to reimburse the additional costs I incurred.

review_text:
Booking.com - SCAM
A few weeks ago, I had one of the worst experiences I’ve ever had as a customer with a global platform: Booking.com

On April 18th, we arrived in Venice at the accommodation we had booked… and it simply did not exist. It was a scam.

At that point, what you expect from a platform like Booking.com is clear:
immediate support, relocation, and a solution.

None of that happened.

Here’s what followed:
There is no live chat, making it extremely difficult to get help in real time
We tried contacting them multiple times and no ticket number was ever generated.

The first two times, we received automated emails asking for more information, but when we replied, the emails bounced back as “no-reply”.

When we finally got through, the response was:

→ “You need to make a new booking on your own”
→ “The refund will be processed later (7–21 days)”

In other words: figure it out yourself.

Booking.com
Did not resolve the situation in real time
Did not offer relocation
Did not provide immediate credit to rebook within the platform
Did not cover the price difference
Did not properly follow up on the case

When you need support the most, the platform simply didn’t respond.

review_text:
Terrible Experience – Would Not Recommend
I booked The Crown London Hotel in good faith, expecting a decent standard of service for a hotel claiming a central London location. Unfortunately, the entire stay turned out to be extremely disappointing.
For starters, the hotel does not even provide a basic facility like a working lift for guests. The only lift available was completely occupied for hotel maintenance, with no alternative arrangements or assistance offered. This is totally unacceptable, especially for families, elderly guests, or anyone travelling with luggage.
Parking was another frustration — there is no proper kiosk or clear system for paying for car parking, leaving guests confused and inconvenienced. For a hotel of this size, this level of disorganisation is simply shocking.
I was travelling with my family and had booked two rooms, reasonably expecting them to be on the same floor. Instead, the rooms were assigned on different floors and separate locations, making it incredibly inconvenient and uncomfortable. Staff showed no effort to accommodate a very basic and logical request.

review_text: Like Amazon, Temu is processing products from remote suppliers. The service it provides has in my experience always been of a very high standard, but the products not always so much. They are sourced in China (as are many on Amazon), where a business approach that lacks integrity seems to be acceptable, and while the product that you receive is often excellent value for money, sometimes it’s substantially inferior to it’s portrayal on the website. I have comfortable, well designed, well made and robust day-to-day casual footwear for instance that would cost double or even treble for something similar elsewhere. I also have items of clothing that had to go straight to landfill. However, Temu does respond fairly and efficiently to justified complaints and overall the good outweighs the bad.

review_text:
Do not EVER buy expensive electronics from Temu! I purchased what was supposed to be a brand new iPhone 17. When it arrived, I set up my camera and lighting to film an unboxing video for my TikTok. Imagine my absolute horror when I broke the factory seals on camera, lifted the lid, and found a literal pile of uncooked grocery store rice instead of a smartphone!

You would think that having undeniable, uncut video evidence of this blatant mail fraud would make getting a refund easy. Absolutely not.

Temu’s robotic customer service is a complete joke. First, they told me that to get my money back, I had to print a return label, box up the uncooked rice, and mail it back to their warehouse. When I refused to waste my time and gas returning garbage from their compromised supply chain, they had the absolute nerve to email me saying they reviewed the order and it was "packed and shipped in alignment with the specifications." They are literally trying to claim a pile of rice meets the specs of an iPhone 17.

I am now having to escalate this to my bank for a fraud chargeback, issue a formal Letter Before Action under the Consumer Rights Act 2015, and report them to Action Fraud and Trading Standards.

Save yourself the massive headache and lost money. Their supply chain is clearly compromised by thieves, and their customer service will do everything to avoid taking responsibility. Avoid this company at all costs!

Despite explaining that this delay has severely hindered my home furnishing plans for my upcoming wedding, customer service continues to send automated, robotic templates. They refuse to escalate the matter to a supervisor or provide any fair compensation for the price difference their negligence caused. Offering a simple refund or asking me to "wait longer" for an out-of-stock item is an insult to the customer. I demand a serious resolution that covers my material damages immediately.

review_text:
I am writing to formally express my frustration and complete dissatisfaction with the handling of my order and the extremely poor level of customer service I have received from SHEIN.

On the 21st of April, I received an email from CTT confirming that my package had arrived at the collection point (Locky Repsol). On the same day, I also received the collection PIN and QR code from Locky. However, when I opened the locker, the package inside was addressed to a completely different person. After checking the contents, it was clear that none of the items belonged to me.

I reported this issue to SHEIN customer support on the 22nd of April, providing full evidence, including photos of the incorrectly addressed package, as well as the emails from both CTT and Locky.

Since then, the handling of this case has been unacceptable.

I have repeatedly received generic responses such as:
“Our team is still actively looking into the issue… please wait 4 days for an update.”

It has now been over 10 days, and I have received no follow-up via email or SMS as promised.

Further responses have been inconsistent and dismissive:
- On the 25th, I was told the case would be escalated.
- On the 28th, I was told the case was under review.
- Then I received an unacceptable and illogical response claiming: “After verification, your package did not contain the wrong goods,” which directly contradicts the evidence I already provided.

This clearly shows that no proper investigation has been conducted.

Additionally, every time I contact support, I am connected to a different agent who has not reviewed the case history, forcing me to repeat the entire situation over and over again. This is extremely frustrating and a complete waste of my time.

To make matters worse, the chat service is not truly live. My last interaction was on the 1st of May at 10:54 AM, and I am still waiting for a response. This level of delay is unacceptable for a customer support system.

At this point, I am left without my order, without a resolution, and without any confidence in your support process.

This entire experience has been extremely frustrating and disappointing, and it reflects very poorly on SHEIN’s customer service standards.

review_text:
I have been a regular online shopper, spending upwards of €200 per month across platforms like Shein and Temu. Unfortunately, my recent experiences with Shein suggest a clear decline in both product reliability and customer service standards. This is the second significant issue I have encountered, and I intend to close my account permanently once I have received my pending items and outstanding refunds.

The latest issue concerns two tinted lipsticks I purchased, based on the product descriptions and what I now suspect may be misleading, compensated reviews. Both were advertised as long-lasting products from the “Shaglam” line. Upon testing, one tint faded completely within 15 minutes, the other within approximately 40 minutes. Furthermore, one product caused a dry, irritated sensation on my lips (though no visible rash), while the other a peel-off tint left red residue on my teeth, which is both unpleasant and unhygienic.

When I contacted customer service to seek a resolution, the experience was deeply unprofessional. The first agent asked me to reapply the products and provide photographic evidenc, despite the fact that sensations like dryness and irritation cannot be captured in images. I complied anyway, providing timestamped videos and photos. A subsequent agent took up to 15 minutes to respond between messages and processed a return without answering my specific questions regarding refund eligibility for used items. Another agent struggled to understand the situation and finally informed me that my query would be escalated to a specialized department.

This entire process has been unnecessarily exhausting. Under Spanish consumer law, consumers have the right to return any online purchase within 14 days of receipt, even if the product has been opened or used. Shein’s refusal to handle this straightforward matter efficiently reflects a broader disregard for customer rights.

A simple solution would be to provide small product samplers, allowing customers to test items without opening the original packaging. Instead, I am left considering legal action, not because the monetary value of this purchase warrants it, but because I am no longer willing to tolerate the way large companies mistreat their customers.

review_text:
I’m raising a formal complaint about inconsistent and unfair currency disclosure in your app.

When I previously used another region, I was clearly notified that pricing was in USD before payment. This gave me the opportunity to cancel. In contrast, using the Australian app, the checkout displayed $79.24 with no clear indication that the price was in USD or that a conversion would apply. I was then charged $110.83.

This inconsistency is not acceptable. If your system can clearly warn users about foreign currency when it results in lower apparent pricing, there is no justification for failing to provide the same level of transparency when it results in a higher charge.

In Australia, the “$” symbol is reasonably understood to mean AUD. Failing to clearly label USD pricing or provide a conversion estimate before payment is misleading and prevents informed consent.

At minimum, your app should:

Explicitly label all non-AUD prices as USD (or relevant currency)
Provide a conversion estimate before payment
Apply this consistently across all regions and scenarios

This is not just a minor UX issue—it directly affects how much customers are charged and whether they can make an informed decision. The current implementation creates a situation where users are only clearly warned when it benefits them, but not when it costs them more.

I expect this to be addressed and escalated internally.

— Proof of payment
— Order screenshots
— Unboxing video
— Photos of the packaging
— CCTV footage of the AliExpress-arranged courier collecting the goods from my address
— The official return tracking number AliExpress themselves provided
— Tracking confirmation from the courier company that the goods were delivered back to AliExpress

I then asked AliExpress five direct, specific questions in a public update:
1. What document type is missing?
2. What format must it be in?
3. Where must it be submitted?
4. What case ID does it apply to?
5. Why is the courier company's own confirmation of return delivery not sufficient?

AliExpress did not answer any of these. They posted the same automated phrase again.

I am writing this as a fresh review specifically because AliExpress ignores updates on existing reviews, but their automated reply system responds to every new review on the page. So this review exists for one reason: to force them to publicly answer the five questions above, or to publicly demonstrate — yet again — that they cannot.

There are only two possibilities:

(a) AliExpress will reply with the same templated "lack of valid evidence" line, refusing to specify anything. In that case, every future buyer reading this Trustpilot page now has documented proof that the phrase is a stock excuse used to deny legitimate refunds.

(b) AliExpress will finally name the specific missing evidence — in which case I will provide it within 24 hours, since every reasonable form of evidence has already been provided and any "missing" item must be something they have invented.

The goods are with AliExpress. Confirmed by their own courier and the shipping company's tracking system.
The money is with AliExpress. Confirmed by my bank statement.
The refund has not been issued. Confirmed by the absence of any credit on my card.

This is the simplest customer dispute imaginable, and AliExpress has chosen to handle it with templated public lies for over 50 days.

Future buyers: if AliExpress ever sends you a courier to return goods, screenshot every step. Save tracking confirmations. Keep CCTV. None of it will get you a refund — but at least you will have a paper trail when the bank chargeback happens.

review_text:
Unfortunately, AliExpress Customer Care at the highest level (Senior Team) supports fraudulent sellers, depriving buyers of any real protection.

I received an item that did not match the description. During the dispute, AliExpress ruled for a "Return and Refund," explicitly stating: "Return shipping paid by: Seller." According to their own policies, this fee is charged to the seller only when "there is reasonable evidence that the item is not as per the specifications claimed by the seller at the time of purchase."

The seller had 3 days to provide a return address, otherwise, I would automatically receive a full refund. To avoid this, the seller provided an address containing the city name followed by a continuous 55-character string of random letters and numbers. No international courier will accept such nonsense for delivery.

I contacted 1st-line chat support 5 or 6 times. Every single agent agreed with me, officially stating in the chat:

"The return address provided by the seller is invalid."

"You must NOT ship the item."

"You will NOT be penalized for not returning it."

They promised to escalate my case to the higher tier. However, the Senior Team repeatedly sabotages the resolution. They keep demanding that I return the item and officially provided me with this "updated" address: "China Guangdong Province Dongguan Humen Town".

That is the entire address. Humen Town is a city of 1 million people. There is no street, no building, and no specific recipient. Who exactly am I supposed to ship this to?

It is physically impossible to return the item to either the seller's 55-character gibberish address or the Senior Team's incomplete address. By demanding the impossible, the Senior Team effectively blocked my refund, despite acknowledging the seller's fraudulent actions.

This is deliberate sabotage by the AliExpress Senior Team to protect a scammer. Beware of shopping on AliExpress—you will be left without your money and without support.

It is a huge shame I cannot attach screenshots here. I have an enormous amount of documented evidence proving every single word of this review!

Continuing Saga of a delivery sent to the wrong address: If I were anyone looking to buy from AliExpress, a word of warning, personally, I wouldn't. The chances of the courier who AliExpress use to deliver your parcel correctly, is probably less than 20%. Yodel, who AliExpress use are totally useless, they dropped off my parcel to the wrong address. Now, both AliExpress and Yodel are passing the buck onto one another. This was the email I received from AliExpress, it was pathetic.

Dear Customer,

We sincerely apologize for the unpleasant experience you’ve had with your order (3071563398080233) due to the unsuccessful delivery.

According to the logistics information, the package status is currently marked as "Delivered," but the specific delivery address is not clearly indicated. In accordance with our platform’s dispute resolution policy, we recommend that you first contact your local post office or courier company to further verify the actual delivery details—such as whether it was received by a neighbor, placed in a mailbox, or failed to be delivered for any other reason.

If it is confirmed that the package was indeed not received or has been lost, kindly request an official **Non-Delivery Certificate** or **Parcel Loss Confirmation** from the relevant carrier. The document should include the following information:

- Tracking number of the parcel
- A clear statement confirming that the package was not successfully delivered or has been lost
- Official seal, agency logo, or electronic signature as valid authentication
- If provided via email, please include a full screenshot showing the sender’s email address, subject line, and message body

Once we receive a valid and complete proof document, we will immediately submit it for review and expedite the refund or compensation process accordingly.

For your convenience, below are the recommended contact details for the local logistics service provider:

Thank you sincerely for your understanding and patience throughout this process. Should you have any questions at any time, please feel free to reply directly to this email—we are always here to support you.

Wishing you all the best in life and smooth sailing in everything you do!

Best regards,
AliExpress Customer Service Team

So, I have to jump through hoops, having already spent a great deal of time, both on the telephone and via email to make sense of the whole thing, my time, not theirs, they are incompetent, firstly for using YODEL and second by not accepting that the company they use is responsible. I have to do all the work. I won't be using AliExpress again, and if you are thinking of using them yourself. JUST LOOK AT THE REVIEWS!!

I am extremely dissatisfied with my experience. Listen to all of the bad reviews.

I did not receive my package, yet this has turned into a never-ending cycle of poor customer service, repeated case closures, and no proper resolution. I have contacted customer service multiple times and repeatedly explained the same issue. Each time, I was asked to provide documentation from the courier, even though I made it clear from the beginning that I could not do that without first being given the courier’s correct contact details.

Despite explaining this countless times, my case was closed more than once without being properly reviewed. I was even told at one stage that the matter would be escalated to higher supervisors because my case had not been properly read or actioned, yet the same thing happened again.

After repeated requests, I was finally given the courier information and contacted them myself, but I received no response. The seller also stated they were contacting the courier, and I received no update from them either. So despite doing everything I reasonably could on my side, I was still left without a refund and without a proper resolution.

This whole process has been exhausting, frustrating, and completely unacceptable. I have spent far too much time chasing customer service, repeating myself, and being sent around in circles over an order I did not receive.

At this stage, I have lost confidence in both the service and the dispute process. I am now taking this further and seeking legal proceedings due to the way this matter has been handled.

I would strongly advise others to listen to the countless bad reviews.

I was sent the incorrect part, the seller even admitted this, i provided clear evidence of this with pictures, a video and even screen shots and i tried to label everything and show the evidence in the pic to make it easy to see, but Ali express has no common sense, all they are paid to do is say "No we dont see anything wrong, sometimes so quick you know they didnt even look at the evidence provided, just automatically  denied. the "Help desk" is useless, a agent asks you to provided everything again which take a huge amount of time, sometimes disappearing mid conversation so your stuck explaining everything once again to another agent, i don't understand how they cant just look at the case number for all the info, i guess they make you do this every time to try waste your time in the hopes you'll just give up and let them keep your hard earned money, after 6 weeks of this, i received an email saying my video can be excepted as evidence and all i need to provided now is the shipping label, but instead of being able to just reply to the email with it they have asked instead to reopen the "after-sales dispute" and start the whole process again from the start, After leaving a negative review of this whole situation i was told via email, that i now need to contact the seller my self to sort the refund, the seller is from china and has very bad English, all he could offer me was a $10 refund!!! they then said they will send me the correct bracket to make it work if i order something else off their website (There trying to avoid paying for freight to put something right) how can Ali Express allow their sellers to do such a thing? ive lost $165NZD, what a scam 

So as a recap.

1- The description stated the part fits all YZ250F dirt bikes between the years of 2010-2023 (My bike is a 2019)
2- there seller supplied me with the incorrect item and even admitted this and suggested i file for a refund
3- i sent Ali express the screen shot of this conversation (Not enough evidence)
4- I provided multiple very clear pictures showing it does not fit (Not enough evidence)
5- I was then asked to provide a video, so i once aging spent 20min removing parts of my bike to show it doesn't fit
6- after providing the video via email i received a reply almost instantly that the evidence was insufficient, basically automatically denied without even viewing the video
7- later on i was told the video can be excepted as evidence but just a shipping label is now needed
8- i provided the shipping label via the email where they asked for it, but was told i need to start the whole after-sales dispute process once again from scratch on the website
9- i went to the "help desk" today and after explaining the whole process and provided all the info once again not once but twice to 2 different agents i received a automated messaged form the second agent saying "not enough evidence"

No common sense, No accountability, No customer service, please do not use this service its a scam

Edit:  Ali expresses will reply to this review saying "I have noticed that your dedicated customer service representative is currently handling your case. Due to the complexity of the situation, it may take a bit longer to resolve. However, I have already urged them to respond to your email as soon as possible. Please continue to communicate with your dedicated customer service via email for further updates"

But its just more lies, ive been told this over and over for the past 4 weeks and nobody has been in contact with me at all, they just send this automated replay to make it look like there trying to help get this sorted for anyone who looks at this review, please be a wear they are making no real attempt to help their customer when ripped off by their suppliers 

Save your self the headache and use Amazon or Ebay instead
